verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat autem et ad discipulos suos: Homo quidam erat dives, qui habebat villicum: et hic diffamatus est apud illum quasi dissipasset bona ipsius.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕλεγεν δὲ καὶ πρὸς τοὺς μαθητάς αὐτοῦ ἄνθρωπός τις ἦν πλούσιος ὃς εἶχεν οἰκονόμον καὶ οὗτος διεβλήθη αὐτῷ ὡς διασκορπίζων τὰ ὑπάρχοντα αὐτοῦ,
verses.king_james: And he said also unto his disciples, There was a certain rich man, which had a steward; and the same was accused unto him that he had wasted his goods.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vocavit illum, et ait illi: Quid hoc audio de te? redde rationem villicationis tuæ: jam enim non poteris villicare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ φωνήσας αὐτὸν εἶπεν αὐτῷ τί τοῦτο ἀκούω περὶ σοῦ ἀπόδος τὸν λόγον τῆς οἰκονομίας σου οὐ γὰρ δύνήσῃ ἔτι οἰκονομεῖν
verses.king_james: And he called him, and said unto him, How is it that I hear this of thee? give an account of thy stewardship; for thou mayest be no longer steward.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem villicus intra se: Quid faciam, quia dominus meus aufert a me villicationem? Fodere non valeo, mendicare erubesco.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ἐν ἑαυτῷ ὁ οἰκονόμος τί ποιήσω ὅτι ὁ κύριός μου ἀφαιρεῖται τὴν οἰκονομίαν ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ σκάπτειν οὐκ ἰσχύω ἐπαιτεῖν αἰσχύνομαι
verses.king_james: Then the steward said within himself, What shall I do? for my lord taketh away from me the stewardship: I cannot dig; to beg I am ashamed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scio quid faciam, ut, cum amotus fuero a villicatione, recipiant me in domos suas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔγνων τί ποιήσω ἵνα ὅταν μετασταθῶ τῆς οἰκονομίας δέξωνταί με εἰς τοὺς οἴκους αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: I am resolved what to do, that, when I am put out of the stewardship, they may receive me into their houses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Convocatis itaque singulis debitoribus domini sui, dicebat primo: Quantum debes domino meo?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος ἕνα ἕκαστον τῶν χρεωφειλετῶν τοῦ κυρίου ἑαυτοῦ ἔλεγεν τῷ πρώτῳ πόσον ὀφείλεις τῷ κυρίῳ μου
verses.king_james: So he called every one of his lord's debtors *unto him*, and said unto the first, How much owest thou unto my lord?

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille dixit: Centum cados olei. Dixitque illi: Accipe cautionem tuam: et sede cito, scribe quinquaginta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ἑκατὸν βάτους ἐλαίου καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ δέξαι σου τὸ γράμμα, καὶ καθίσας ταχέως γράψον πεντήκοντα
verses.king_james: And he said, An hundred measures of oil. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and sit down quickly, and write fifty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deinde alii dixit: Tu vero quantum debes? Qui ait: Centum coros tritici. Ait illi: Accipe litteras tuas, et scribe octoginta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔπειτα ἑτέρῳ εἶπεν σὺ δὲ πόσον ὀφείλεις ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ἑκατὸν κόρους σίτου καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ δέξαι σου τὸ γράμμα, καὶ γράψον ὀγδοήκοντα
verses.king_james: Then said he to another, And how much owest thou? And he said, An hundred measures of wheat. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and write fourscore.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et laudavit dominus villicum iniquitatis, quia prudenter fecisset: quia filii hujus sæculi prudentiores filiis lucis in generatione sua sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπῄνεσεν ὁ κύριος τὸν οἰκονόμον τῆς ἀδικίας ὅτι φρονίμως ἐποίησεν ὅτι οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου φρονιμώτεροι ὑπὲρ τοὺς υἱοὺς τοῦ φωτὸς εἰς τὴν γενεὰν τὴν ἑαυτῶν εἰσιν
verses.king_james: And the lord commended the unjust steward, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego vobis dico: facite vobis amicos de mammona iniquitatis: ut, cum defeceritis, recipiant vos in æterna tabernacula.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κα᾽γὼ ὑμῖν λέγω ποιήσατε ἑαυτοῖς φίλους ἐκ τοῦ μαμωνᾶ τῆς ἀδικίας ἵνα ὅταν ἐκλίπητε, δέξωνται ὑμᾶς εἰς τὰς αἰωνίους σκηνάς
verses.king_james: And I say unto you, Make to yourselves friends of the mammon of unrighteousness; that, when ye fail, they may receive you into everlasting habitations.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui fidelis est in minimo, et in majori fidelis est: et qui in modico iniquus est, et in majori iniquus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ πιστὸς ἐν ἐλαχίστῳ καὶ ἐν πολλῷ πιστός ἐστιν καὶ ὁ ἐν ἐλαχίστῳ ἄδικος καὶ ἐν πολλῷ ἄδικός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: He that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much: and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo in iniquo mammona fideles non fuistis quod verum est, quis credet vobis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ οὖν ἐν τῷ ἀδίκῳ μαμωνᾷ πιστοὶ οὐκ ἐγένεσθε τὸ ἀληθινὸν τίς ὑμῖν πιστεύσει
verses.king_james: If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true *riches*?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si in alieno fideles non fuistis, quod vestrum est, quis dabit vobis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰ ἐν τῷ ἀλλοτρίῳ πιστοὶ οὐκ ἐγένεσθε τὸ ὑμέτερον τίς ὑμῖν δώσει
verses.king_james: And if ye have not been faithful in that which is another man's, who shall give you that which is your own?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo servus potest duobus dominis servire: aut enim unum odiet, et alterum diliget: aut uni adhærebit, et alterum contemnet. Non potestis Deo servire et mammonæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδεὶς οἰκέτης δύναται δυσὶ κυρίοις δουλεύειν ἢ γὰρ τὸν ἕνα μισήσει καὶ τὸν ἕτερον ἀγαπήσει ἢ ἑνὸς ἀνθέξεται καὶ τοῦ ἑτέρου καταφρονήσει οὐ δύνασθε θεῷ δουλεύειν καὶ μαμωνᾷ
verses.king_james: No servant can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audiebant autem omnia hæc pharisæi, qui erant avari: et deridebant illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤκουον δὲ ταῦτα πάντα καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι φιλάργυροι ὑπάρχοντες καὶ ἐξεμυκτήριζον αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Vos estis qui justificatis vos coram hominibus: Deus autem novit corda vestra: quia quod hominibus altum est, abominatio est ante Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὑμεῖς ἐστε οἱ δικαιοῦντες ἑαυτοὺς ἐνώπιον τῶν ἀνθρώπων ὁ δὲ θεὸς γινώσκει τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν ὅτι τὸ ἐν ἀνθρώποις ὑψηλὸν βδέλυγμα ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lex et prophetæ usque ad Joannem: ex eo regnum Dei evangelizatur, et omnis in illud vim facit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ νόμος καὶ οἱ προφῆται ἕως ἰωάννου ἀπὸ τότε ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ εὐαγγελίζεται καὶ πᾶς εἰς αὐτὴν βιάζεται
verses.king_james: The law and the prophets *were* until John: since that time the kingdom of God is preached, and every man presseth into it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facilius est autem cælum et terram præterire, quam de lege unum apicem cadere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐκοπώτερον δέ ἐστιν τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν παρελθεῖν ἢ τοῦ νόμου μίαν κεραίαν πεσεῖν
verses.king_james: And it is easier for heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of the law to fail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis qui dimittit uxorem suam et alteram ducit, mœchatur: et qui dimissam a viro ducit, mœchatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς ὁ ἀπολύων τὴν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ καὶ γαμῶν ἑτέραν μοιχεύει καὶ πᾶς ὁ ἀπολελυμένην ἀπὸ ἀνδρὸς γαμῶν μοιχεύει
verses.king_james: Whosoever putteth away his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and whosoever marrieth her that is put away from *her* husband committeth adultery.

verses.latin_vulgate: Homo quidam erat dives, qui induebatur purpura et bysso, et epulabatur quotidie splendide.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄνθρωπος δέ τις ἦν πλούσιος καὶ ἐνεδιδύσκετο πορφύραν καὶ βύσσον εὐφραινόμενος καθ᾽ ἡμέραν λαμπρῶς
verses.king_james: There was a certain rich man, which was clothed in purple and fine linen, and fared sumptuously every day:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat quidam mendicus, nomine Lazarus, qui jacebat ad januam ejus, ulceribus plenus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πτωχὸς δέ τις ἦν ὀνόματι λάζαρος ὃς ἐβέβλητο πρὸς τὸν πυλῶνα αὐτοῦ ἡλκωμένος
verses.king_james: And there was a certain beggar named Lazarus, which was laid at his gate, full of sores,

verses.latin_vulgate: cupiens saturari de micis quæ cadebant de mensa divitis, et nemo illi dabat: sed et canes veniebant, et lingebant ulcera ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπιθυμῶν χορτασθῆναι ἀπὸ τῶν ψιχίων τῶν πιπτόντων ἀπὸ τῆς τραπέζης τοῦ πλουσίου ἀλλὰ καὶ οἱ κύνες ἐρχόμενοι ἀπέλειχον τὰ ἕλκη αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And desiring to be fed with the crumbs which fell from the rich man's table: moreover the dogs came and licked his sores.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem ut moreretur mendicus, et portaretur ab angelis in sinum Abrahæ. Mortuus est autem et dives, et sepultus est in inferno.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ ἀποθανεῖν τὸν πτωχὸν καὶ ἀπενεχθῆναι αὐτὸν ὑπὸ τῶν ἀγγέλων εἰς τὸν κόλπον τοῦ ἀβραάμ ἀπέθανεν δὲ καὶ ὁ πλούσιος καὶ ἐτάφη
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that the beggar died, and was carried by the angels into Abraham's bosom: the rich man also died, and was buried;

verses.latin_vulgate: Elevans autem oculos suos, cum esset in tormentis, vidit Abraham a longe, et Lazarum in sinu ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν τῷ ᾅδῃ ἐπάρας τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς αὐτοῦ ὑπάρχων ἐν βασάνοις ὁρᾷ τὸν ἀβραὰμ ἀπὸ μακρόθεν καὶ λάζαρον ἐν τοῖς κόλποις αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And in hell he lift up his eyes, being in torments, and seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ipse clamans dixit: Pater Abraham, miserere mei, et mitte Lazarum ut intingat extremum digiti sui in aquam, ut refrigeret linguam meam, quia crucior in hac flamma.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς φωνήσας εἶπεν πάτερ ἀβραάμ ἐλέησόν με καὶ πέμψον λάζαρον ἵνα βάψῃ τὸ ἄκρον τοῦ δακτύλου αὐτοῦ ὕδατος καὶ καταψύξῃ τὴν γλῶσσάν μου ὅτι ὀδυνῶμαι ἐν τῇ φλογὶ ταύτῃ
verses.king_james: And he cried and said, Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue; for I am tormented in this flame.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit illi Abraham: Fili, recordare quia recepisti bona in vita tua, et Lazarus similiter mala: nunc autem hic consolatur, tu vero cruciaris:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ἀβραάμ τέκνον μνήσθητι ὅτι ἀπέλαβες σὺ τὰ ἀγαθά σου ἐν τῇ ζωῇ σου καὶ λάζαρος ὁμοίως τὰ κακά νῦν δὲ ὅδε παρακαλεῖται σὺ δὲ ὀδυνᾶσαι
verses.king_james: But Abraham said, Son, remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things: but now he is comforted, and thou art tormented.

verses.latin_vulgate: et in his omnibus inter nos et vos chaos magnum firmatum est: ut hi qui volunt hinc transire ad vos, non possint, neque inde huc transmeare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπὶ πάσιν τούτοις μεταξὺ ἡμῶν καὶ ὑμῶν χάσμα μέγα ἐστήρικται ὅπως οἱ θέλοντες διαβῆναι ἐντεῦθεν πρὸς ὑμᾶς μὴ δύνωνται μηδὲ οἱ ἐκεῖθεν πρὸς ἡμᾶς διαπερῶσιν
verses.king_james: And beside all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed: so that they which would pass from hence to you cannot; neither can they pass to us, that *would come* from thence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait: Rogo ergo te, pater, ut mittas eum in domum patris mei:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δέ ἐρωτῶ οὖν σε πάτερ ἵνα πέμψῃς αὐτὸν εἰς τὸν οἶκον τοῦ πατρός μου
verses.king_james: Then he said, I pray thee therefore, father, that thou wouldest send him to my father's house:

verses.latin_vulgate: habeo enim quinque fratres: ut testetur illis, ne et ipsi veniant in hunc locum tormentorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔχω γὰρ πέντε ἀδελφούς ὅπως διαμαρτύρηται αὐτοῖς ἵνα μὴ καὶ αὐτοὶ ἔλθωσιν εἰς τὸν τόπον τοῦτον τῆς βασάνου
verses.king_james: For I have five brethren; that he may testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illi Abraham: Habent Moysen et prophetas: audiant illos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ἀβραάμ ἔχουσιν μωσέα καὶ τοὺς προφήτας ἀκουσάτωσαν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Abraham saith unto him, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille dixit: Non, pater Abraham: sed si quis ex mortuis ierit ad eos, pœnitentiam agent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν οὐχί πάτερ ἀβραάμ ἀλλ᾽ ἐάν τις ἀπὸ νεκρῶν πορευθῇ πρὸς αὐτοὺς μετανοήσουσιν
verses.king_james: And he said, Nay, father Abraham: but if one went unto them from the dead, they will repent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem illi: Si Moysen et prophetas non audiunt, neque si quis ex mortuis resurrexerit, credent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῷ εἰ μωσέως καὶ τῶν προφητῶν οὐκ ἀκούουσιν οὐδὲ ἐάν τις ἐκ νεκρῶν ἀναστῇ πεισθήσονται
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, If they hear not Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded, though one rose from the dead.
